attacking_style: Attacks from long breakaways on steep finishing climbs with 4-5km remaining, excelling on extreme gradients (20%+). Bridges to or attacks from established breaks rather than initiating from peloton.
team_tactics: BORA lacks consistent firepower to support his ambitions in GC scenarios or create tactical isolation. Functions best as lone ranger in breakaways without requiring team setup.
terrain_preference: Thrives on brutal finishing gradients (20-23%) and sustained diesel efforts under 5km. Exposed on technical circuit finales and when race fragments unpredictably in flatter, chaotic scenarios.
vulnerability: Struggles when races fracture on non-climbing terrain (circuits, technical finales). Poor form periods leave him absent from finales even on suited profiles. Cannot compete in pure sprint scenarios.
key_patterns: Completes stage wins from late attacks out of breakaways, not peloton (2 Grand Tour wins).
Attacks with under 5km remaining on steep gradients after extended efforts (Vuelta, Tour examples).
BORA repeatedly unable to protect GC ambitions or create numerical advantage (Giro, Tirreno examples).
Extraordinary diesel power on climbs betrayed only by final explosive gradient or competition (Tour 100m loss).
Form inconsistency: absent from suited finales when off-form (Giro 2023 example).
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 Vuelta win confirms climbing ability intact, but earlier 2023 absences (Giro, Tirreno struggles) suggest inconsistent preparation or tactical role confusion
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 9
2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 9
